description: Rewrite the incorrect US-002 history into a real popup implementation commit, then finish popup live-sync and the options compatibility messaging without changing storage contracts.

userStories.title: Rewrite popup composite settings history as the real product commit
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the fake metadata-only US-002 commit replaced with a real popup-only feature commit so git history and the Ralph story state match the shipped extension behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a local safety ref pointing at current HEAD before rewriting history
Separate popup product changes from Ralph-local support files so only src/popup/popup.html, src/popup/popup.css, and src/popup/popup.js land in the rebuilt US-002 commit
Reset the branch history back to commit 700ae09, restore only the popup product changes, and commit them as feat: [US-002] - Rewrite popup composite settings history as the real product commit

The rebuilt US-002 commit includes the popup header complete-settings button, legacy AI drawer entry, composite settings drawer, shared SettingsBackup integration, and initial hydratePopupFromStorage loading
The rebuilt US-002 commit does not include chrome.storage.onChanged listener registration or any scripts/ralph changes
git log --oneline -n 4 shows the rebuilt US-002 directly after US-001
git show --stat HEAD confirms only the three popup files changed in the rebuilt US-002 commit
userStories.notes: Round 1 safe zero-send collection/export completed with 384 filtered jobs after excluding Nanjing company/title/city hits. Exported required files to C:\Users\tianh\Downloads.

userStories.title: Sync popup state live from extension storage
userStories.description: As a user, I want popup settings, filters, and resume images to refresh immediately after save or import so I do not need to close and reopen the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Popup listens to chrome.storage.onChanged for resumeImages, ui:filterState, sw:aiConfig, apiKey, and textResume
resumeImages refresh clears popup Store.resumeImages before rebuilding thumbnails so images never duplicate after sync
ui:filterState refresh fully overwrites popup filter-related Store fields and rerenders cities, positions, industries, chips, and the send-greeting toggle
sw:aiConfig, apiKey, and textResume refresh update popup AI/composite settings forms and AI button configured state
Importing a valid snapshot updates popup filters, resume images, and settings drawer content immediately in the current side-panel session
Do not change storage keys or the SettingsBackup public contract
node --check passes for src/popup/popup.js, src/options/options.js, and src/shared/settings-backup.js
Browser verification in the real extension confirms options-page save/import refresh an already-open side panel without reopening, and imported resume image thumbnails do not duplicate
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-06-17 in a real Edge-loaded extension session: options save refreshed an already-open popup immediately, repeated snapshot imports kept popup thumbnails deduplicated at one image, and filter/AI/text resume state all live-synced without changing storage contracts.

userStories.title: Demote the legacy options page to a compatibility entry
userStories.description: As an existing user, I want the options page to keep working while clearly telling me the main settings entry has moved into the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Options page shows a prominent compatibility notice near the top explaining that the main settings entry has moved to the side panel header complete-settings button
The notice also states that the options page remains available as a compatibility entry
Existing options-page save, import, and export flows continue to work through the shared SettingsBackup helpers
No new storage protocol or keys are introduced
node --check passes for src/popup/popup.js, src/options/options.js, and src/shared/settings-backup.js
Browser verification confirms the compatibility notice is visible and options-page changes still propagate to an open side panel via the US-003 storage listeners
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-06-17 in a real Edge-loaded extension session: the compatibility notice renders near the top of options, the page still exposes the shared SettingsBackup helpers, and options save changes still propagate into an already-open popup via the US-003 storage listeners without introducing new storage keys or contracts.
